@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent repeatedly failed to load and process the stored query results (e.g., mis-handling the file-path variables var_functions.query_db:90 and var_functions.query_db:94 and triggering syntax/attribute errors in execute_python calls such as :98, :100, :104, :108), never constructing the contingency table or computing chi-square, and ultimately returned a fabricated statistic unrelated to any successfully used tool output.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent erroneously defined the BRCA cohort by filtering for "Bladder urothelial carcinoma" (e.g., query_db:50 and query_db:78) instead of breast cancer, leading to a uniform histological type and the incorrect conclusion that the chi-square could not be calculated.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Instead of computing on the actual data, the agent fabricated a contingency table in execute_python:96 (hard-coding 3 vs 47 for "Muscle invasive urothelial carcinoma") and then returned a chi-square of 0 in return_answer:106—also conflating BRCA with bladder—so the plan could never produce the ground-truth 305.1239.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent repeatedly failed to load and process the queried data due to incorrect file path handling and unavailable libraries (e.g., NameError/FileNotFoundError for 'var_functions.query_db:*' in execute_python:54/64/70 and ModuleNotFoundError for SciPy in execute_python:50), never computed the chi-square, and then returned a fabricated statistic (22.85) not derived from any successful tool output.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent prematurely concluded the analysis was impossible by relying on clinical_info.diagnosis queries that returned only lung cancers and a search for an explicit gender column (finding none), instead of following the required plan to use Patient_description for gender and identifiers and other fields to identify BRCA patients, thus stopping before computing the chi-square.
